basics.name: Benjamin Armus
basics.label: Historian
basics.summary: PhD student in History at the University of Texas at Austin, specializing in early modern English intellectual and institutional history. Research focuses on the transformation of university pedagogy, disputation, and scholastic logic at Oxford and Cambridge.

work.name: Gale Academic
work.summary: Wrote a digital humanities research article using the Gale Digital Scholar Lab for the Gale Research Showcase. Created blog and social media content highlighting archival materials. Organized an on-campus event promoting Gale resources and engaged in professional development through weekly skill-building sessions.

work.name: William & Mary Residence Life
work.summary: Peer leader for approximately 20-30 first-year students in Nicholson Hall, Spotswood Hall, and Gooch Hall. Managed bonding events, enforced University policy, and mentored new Resident Assistants.

education.institution: The University of Texas at Austin

highlights: Alpha Delta Gamma (Medieval and Renaissance Studies Honors)
Studied at Hertford College, University of Oxford, Jan. 2025 - Jun. 2025, tutorials in medieval and early modern history

publications.name: The Transformation of University Pedagogy in Eighteenth Century England
publications.summary: A digital humanities research article using the Gale Digital Scholar Lab. Forthcoming 2026.

publications.name: Crown and Gown: The Death of Religious Disputations at Oxford and Cambridge
'Le Temps Passé n'est Plus:' Transcending the Past and Temporalizing the French Revolution

skills.name: Paleography
keywords: Basic proficiency in reading early modern English handwriting (secretary hand)
